title: China-to-Mauritius Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC Evidence — IEC 61000 Series and IEC 62920 for Mauritius PV Inverter Submissions
rows.target_requirement: Mauritius Standards Bureau (MSB) is the national standards body, and Mauritius practice is IEC/BS-influenced because of its British colonial standards legacy. For PV inverters, EMC evidence should be prepared on an IEC-aligned basis, especially IEC 62920 for photovoltaic power conversion equipment and applicable IEC 61000-series emission and immunity tests. CEB project reviewers and procurement specifications may require these EMC reports as part of the inverter technical package for SSDG/MSDG or larger distributed-generation projects.
target_standards: Mauritius Standards Bureau (MSB) standards and conformity-assessment framework
IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility emission and immunity standards
CEB technical review for distributed-generation connection submissions
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence is commonly built around GB/T 37408-2021 and related domestic test reports from CNAS-accredited laboratories. These reports may show useful design maturity, but they are not automatically treated as IEC 62920 or IEC 61000 compliance evidence for Mauritius submissions unless the report scope, editions, limits, test configuration, and laboratory accreditation are acceptable to the Mauritius project reviewer or conformity body.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC-related requirements
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: GB/T 37408-2021 EMC files should be mapped clause-by-clause against IEC 62920 and the applicable IEC 61000-series tests. Exporters should provide IEC test reports for the exact inverter model, power rating, firmware configuration, accessories, and enclosure options supplied to Mauritius. Where testing was performed only under China domestic conditions, confirm whether Mauritius 230/400 V 50 Hz operation, tropical humidity, coastal salt-mist exposure, and installation layout require supplemental EMC or environmental validation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where MSB conformity, CEB technical review, procurement specifications, or project-owner requirements call for IEC-aligned EMC evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese EMC evidence may support domestic certification or voluntary conformity but is not automatically accepted as Mauritius IEC-aligned EMC evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Prepare IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000-series EMC evidence for Mauritius instead of relying only on GB/T 37408-2021 reports. Confirm with MSB, CEB, the EPC contractor, or project owner whether Chinese CNAS reports are acceptable as supporting evidence or whether ILAC-accredited IEC reports are required for the exact inverter configuration.

rows.requirement: CEB SSDG/MSDG Grid Connection — IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 and 230/400 V 50 Hz Settings
rows.target_requirement: Mauritius rooftop and distributed solar PV projects are connected through Central Electricity Board (CEB) small-scale and medium-scale distributed generation schemes, commonly referred to as SSDG and MSDG. Inverter approval is tied to the CEB connection application, technical review, metering arrangement, commissioning, and project-specific connection conditions. Mauritius uses a 230/400 V, 50 Hz network, nominally aligned with China frequency but not automatically with Chinese protection settings. IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence are the expected IEC-aligned grid documents for PV inverters, together with local CEB settings and any URA-approved scheme conditions.
target_standards: CEB SSDG and MSDG distributed-generation scheme requirements for rooftop and distributed solar PV connection
Utility Regulatory Authority (URA) approval and regulatory oversight for electricity-sector schemes in Mauritius
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Mauritius low-voltage supply context — 230/400 V, 50 Hz
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese inverter grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 for photovoltaic power-station connection, NB/T 32004 inverter technical specifications, GB/T 37408-2021 inverter technical requirements, and domestic anti-islanding or protection tests. These documents are based on Chinese grid-operator practice and Chinese voltage/protection assumptions. They do not create automatic acceptance under Mauritius CEB SSDG/MSDG connection review, even though both countries use 50 Hz nominal frequency.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T and NB/T grid files should be supplemented with IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence, a configuration record for Mauritius 230/400 V 50 Hz protection settings, and the CEB SSDG/MSDG project submission package. Exporters should confirm whether the inverter model is acceptable to CEB for the project size, metering arrangement, and connection point before shipment. China certificates are supporting documents only; they do not replace URA/CEB scheme approval and final CEB connection.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for CEB SSDG/MSDG grid connection and project commissioning; URA/CEB scheme approval and CEB connection are project gates
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies Chinese grid-operator expectations but is not transferable to Mauritius CEB connection approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat CEB SSDG/MSDG approval and connection as the controlling Mauritius grid gate. Chinese GB/T 19964-2024, GB/T 37408-2021, and NB/T 32004 documents are not automatic substitutes for IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 evidence or for CEB project review. Validate inverter firmware and protection settings for Mauritius 230/400 V at 50 Hz before commissioning.

rows.requirement: Safety and Environmental Suitability — IEC 62109, MSB Conformity, Cyclone and Salt-Mist Derating
rows.target_requirement: PV inverter market access and project acceptance in Mauritius should be prepared around MSB conformity expectations and IEC-aligned safety evidence. IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the core safety standards for power converters and PV inverters. Because Mauritius is a developed Indian-Ocean island market with high renewable-energy targets and coastal installations, project files should also address environmental suitability: tropical heat and humidity, cyclone wind exposure through mounting and enclosure choices, salt-mist/corrosion derating for coastal sites, ingress protection, and serviceability under CEB connection conditions.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
Mauritius Standards Bureau (MSB) conformity and standards adoption framework
Mauritius renewable-energy policy context including the national target of 60% renewable energy by 2030
Project-specific coastal, cyclone, salt-mist, ingress-protection, and derating requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, factory quality-control files, CQC or CGC voluntary certificates, and CNAS laboratory reports. These can support a technical dossier but are not automatic substitutes for IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence or for Mauritius project-owner environmental suitability requirements. Chinese standard operation may not cover Mauritius coastal corrosion exposure or cyclone-related installation assumptions unless separately tested or documented.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: A China-only safety dossier should be supplemented with IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence, MSB conformity documentation where applicable, and environmental suitability records for Mauritius. For coastal projects, include salt-mist/corrosion class assumptions, enclosure ingress protection, thermal derating curves, surge protection coordination, installation clearances, and cyclone-resilient mounting interface information. Confirm whether the CEB project reviewer, EPC contractor, insurer, or project owner requires additional tropical or coastal test evidence before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where MSB conformity, CEB technical review, EPC procurement, insurer, or project-owner requirements call for IEC 62109 and environmental suitability evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T and NB/T evidence may support China domestic conformity or voluntary certification but does not automatically satisfy Mauritius IEC and environmental project requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on China-only GB/T 37408-2021 or NB/T 32004 safety evidence for Mauritius project acceptance. Prepare IEC 62109-1/-2 reports and a Mauritius environmental suitability file addressing coastal salt mist, cyclone exposure, humidity, thermal derating, ingress protection, and project-specific CEB or owner requirements.

source.publisher: Ministry of Energy and Public Utilities (Mauritius)
